entry.title: Re: Sync Employee Central Termination Date to S/4 HANA Cloud
entry.content: Added EC tag.

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hello @David_Chaviano, 

I'm Phuoc from Vietnam. Over the past 8 months, I've had the opportunity to work with SAP Business One (SAP B1), where I’ve gained practical experience in both consulting and programming with ABAP. 

What excites me most about SAP is its ability to bring together various aspects of a business into one cohesive system, allowing for better decision-making and efficiency. As I delve deeper into SAP, my curiosity and eagerness to learn more about its advanced functionalities only grow stronger. I’m particularly interested in how SAP can be leveraged to drive innovation and create value within organizations.

Earning an SAP certification is one of my primary career goals. I believe that formal recognition of my skills and knowledge would not only validate my efforts but also open doors to new opportunities and challenges in the field. I am eager to continue my learning journey and to upgrade my skills to stay ahead in this ever-evolving digital landscape.

Thank you for considering my application. I truly appreciate the chance to be a part of this initiative and look forward to the possibility of contributing to and learning from this community.

Best regards,
Phuoc Nguyen Quang

entry.title: Re: SWFVISU - INTENT visualization for WEBGUI based tasks
entry.content: without the tcode in paramter, what happened?

entry.title: Spend Trends September 2024
entry.content:
Summary

A quieter month on the report front, largely because it was summer holidays in the northern hemisphere, but what we lack in quantity, we make up for in quality!

First we start with a very hand buying guide giving insights into a range of categories that will help us understand the issues many of these buyers are facing. Before we look into the range of trends and challenges that companies and procurement face and that for many its time to reinvent their strategy as the disruption and geopolitical climate has impacted existing strategies effectiveness.

One of the main areas that will always be on the table is supplier management and performance, and whilst there are several reports that conflict, the importance is undeniable. If companies want to embark on cost out programs they will need the support of the suppliers and supply chain to be successful.

Finally what does great look like? Well digital great is outlined in the final report, and for me that’s the read of the month!

S&P Q3 Buying Strategies

This was a webinar I attended and one of the resources weas a really interesting guide into a whole variety of commodities and categories of spend and events over last few months.

The commodities include, Energy, Metals, Steels, Building materials and it gives a really good broad understanding of some of the market pressures across the market such as…

 “the two primary causes of this are the divergence of the construction spending outlook for different sectors and the size of price growth throughout the pandemic.”

Whilst this doesn’t cover all of the sub areas, it does give you considerable background into what’s happening broadly and of course you can reach out to S&P for a more detailed review.

AQPC Direct & Indirect Trends

This report provides a cross-industry snapshot of the current state of direct and indirect procurement, including procurement challenges, supplier relationship management, deployment of purchasing application, cloud environment & use of technologies in procurement processes. It surveyed 285 participants in the research, which is a great number.

The report stated that over two-fifths of respondents identified shortages or delays in raw materials and/or cybersecurity risks to be among the top three challenges in procurement currently experienced by their business entities. Complexity in data management is the third most common challenge.

Whilst there was commonalty in the focus areas for supplier relationships, they were inverted depending in direct or indirect. Quality and delivery were the top 2 in that order for directs, whereas for indirect delivery was more important. Interesting to note that price focus was 2% and 1% which is different from many other studies.

Other key takeaways included 71% of orgs tracked tail spend (im surprised its not more).

When it comes to emerging tech, most organisations are in the evaluation phase rather than piloting or implementing, again whereas for more mature tech like RPA, more are in the piloting phase for Directs, but in the considering phase for Indirect. Its interesting how few are in the operating or implementing phase.

CAPS

If you haven’t heard of CAPS they are the Centre for advanced purchasing statistics (right up my street!)

 

They produce a regular stream of research, that is available to members (im not a member sadly) but you can still get the top sheets. This month they have had a couple of top sheets that leads back to a metrics of supply management study

One is an average cost savings per sourcing employee which averages $6.9Million per employee and ranges from 6.3 million for manufacturing, and 8.8 million for services.

 

They also shared their organisational design statistics which has 49% Centre led which is similar to the Economist study showing CoE as the lead followed by Centre Led.

Procuretech – Elevating Supplier Performance

This 100 person study sought to explore the strength, or absence, of current supplier engagement strategies and how they manage their relationships with suppliers to deliver high performance.

Some interesting key takeaways

  • 37% analyse supplier performance on a monthly basis
  • 72% state that supplier performance data has unlocked improvements in supply resilience
  • 100% use at least three tools to integrate performance data.

Over two thirds of respondents (68%) named optimising cost and cashflow as a leading priority. This was shortly followed by strengthening supplier or partner collaboration (55%) and improving environmental and social impact (53%).

Interestingly in contrast to the AQPC report earlier, this report respondents selected commercial and price performance (47%) as the highest ranked importance area for supplier performance, with only 16% focussing on quality and delivery (each).

Another interesting takeaway was that 37% analysed the supplier performance data monthly, with 28% doing so quarterly. One of the biggest improvement strategies  identified was to have regular feedback sessions with their suppliers (59%). I personally was surprised that the monthly review was only 37%, I expected that number to be higher, especially when the report also says 73% said that supplier performance data positively impacted business credibility and 72% said it improved supply resilience. Makes sense then that the top improvement area was to have more regular meetings!

Gartner Business Quarterly

The business quarterly is a great read for getting a handle on how business are reacting to the world around them. Goes into huge amounts of differing areas.

In this quarters “navigating a world without truth” it goes into detail about AI and trust in the technology, how to boost organisational culture, and what are organisations doing to cope with all the disruption.

For this question, basically they are adapting their strategies/ 79% are planning revamped strategies by the end of 2024. Therefore procurement leaders will need to be across these changes, to see how their category strategies will need to pivot to adapt.

AI is a big area (wait what…!) and this report covers it in a number of ways. From identifying where their survey thinks headcount reductions will be (Customer service, finance and product/service ops) to the fact that 84% are engaged in or planning AI initiatives over the next 3 years and to a poll that returned 42% said the biggest AI concern was data privacy.

There was a section on organisational culture. As someone who spent a bit of time studying Charles Handy and understanding organisations for various qualifications, organisational dynamics and culture is an interesting area. Some stats jumped out from this section.

  • Eighty-two percent of board members agree organizational culture is very or extremely important.
  • Only 53% of employees trust their organizations
  • Only 63% of organizations trust their employees

Meaning their may well be mutual mis trust. Trust is critical for buyer seller dynamics as it is for employer/employee and there are some interesting case studies from PayPal, DSM, and Laurentian Bank as to how their organisations seek to build trust and improve culture.

Hackett Digital World Class

This annual study into benchmarking digital world class organisations against their peers, is always well received especially when so many research pieces are call out the digitalisation drive that many organisations are prioritising now.

Digital World Class® procurement organizations run at a 21% lower cost than the peer group, with 32% fewer full-time equivalent (FTE) staff. Yet, they deliver much more: greater spend influence, faster time to value and more focus on value creation. It’s no surprise that they are 1.7 times more likely to be considered a valued business partner. These organizations returned two times more overall cost savings and increased the savings contribution from 2023 to 2024 at a rate that is 4.5 times faster than the peer group.

Digital World Class procurement organizations invest 15% of their total operating budget in technology. A closer look at the composition of procurement operating cost as a percentage of spend highlights a 32% greater technology cost for top-performing procurement organizations as compared to the peer group.

Some other numbers that jumped out to me. Digital world class organisations:

  • Had 21% more spend influenced
  • 15% more likely to involved earlier
  • 59% less savings lost due to maverick spending

It’s a great read and one worth spending the time to go through and consider what it means

 

As always reach out to discuss more, and always happy to hear your thoughts!

entry.title: Re: 3E WWI Template / Chapter 14
entry.content: Hi CB, this is also the case here. We are loading 3E OCC data with default usage PUBLIC/REG_WORLD. However we hit into similar issue with 3E section 14 templates. We had to remove some G group wwi logic there to make it work. We asked 3E years ago if it's really a need to use G group in the template, but did not get a good feedback at that time. But indeed we were told by 3E it's best not to specify usage for identifier data load by 3E. We are still loading the identifers with usage but may re-visit this topic in the future to make it more standard for the preparation of product compliance module.

entry.title: Re: Batch Award Nominations
entry.content:
Hi @KarelynMagat 

I doubt it is possible but I forwarded your request to our Product team. I will follow up with them and update here.

All the best

Xavier

entry.title: Re: SWFVISU - INTENT visualization for WEBGUI based tasks
entry.content: Request is redirected to FLP #Shell-home.

entry.title: Re: Multiple Valuation Approaches/Transfer Prices in SAP S/4 HANA - Implementation hints
entry.content:
Good job!

In your blog, you mentioned the note 2554992 - Multiple valuation approaches/transfer prices: Permitted representative ledgers in Asset Accounting.

For the note 2554992, I have a question :

"In the example 3 of the NOTE 2554992 complaint, the 0L2L Ledger group contains both 0L and 2L ledgers, and depreciation areas 01 and 15 are posted to the 0L2L Ledger group, so that depreciation areas 01 and 15 are posted to both the 0L ledger and the 2L ledger. Will this result in documents relating to asset postings (e.g. asset depreciation) in either the 0L or 2L ledger data containing data for both depreciation areas, i.e. will this result in duplicate data? How can this be avoided? However, I think in this scenario, the ledger 0L would only need to post data in the 01 depreciation area; and 2L would only need to post data in the 15 depreciation area."

What do you think about this? @AlinaVranceanu , @FilipeMag 

Thanks and regards!

entry.title: Q)Adjustment of production orders' scheduled time in HANA cloud is disabled
entry.content:
Dear community members,

 

I hope you are doing well.

An issue was found while I was testing production order integration with SAP Digital Manufacturing(MES). In app Create Production Order, scheduled time box cannot be adjusted. Then, it confirms without any adjustments after release. Related picture below:

Furthermore, parameters for scheduling such as Setup Time; Processing Time; Teardown Time; etc. of each operation cannot be maintained. Related picture below:

For your information, my team is using SAP DM TDD & Hana Cloud Partner Demo Customizing. I expect that I am missing some vital master data set.  

I appreciate your comment with opinion in advance.

 

Yours faithfully,

Wonpyo

#production order #scheduling order #create order

entry.title: Re: Integrating SAP S/4HANA Data into SAP HANA Cloud Using SAP Datasphere
entry.content: Using Datasphere Replication Flow entails incurring Datasphere costs of Premium Outbound Integration (POI) and Data Integration options, versus Smart Data Integration (SDI, mentioned in the Current Position section of the discovery mission) which is an component of HANA Cloud HANA Database, or SLT. Can you comment on pros and cons of using Datasphere Replication Flow versus SDI/SLT for SAP ERP to HANA Cloud HANA Database replication?

entry.title: Re: 專業服務專案開票問題
entry.content:
Hi

What kind of PR is this, material PR or Service PR?

And I believe you're getting this error while accessing the PR, kindly confirm in which application you're getting this error.

Regards,

Sharan

entry.title: Re: Direct Amount - Total Eligible Base
entry.content:
Hi @pwc_94 

Direct Amount budgets can only be used with a feature called Budget Cascading, please see this blog. 

https://community.sap.com/t5/human-capital-management-blogs-by-members/understand-budget-type-direct-amount-in-successfactors-compensation/ba-p/13396127

Additionally please see my answer to this post: 

https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-successfactors-compensation/compensation-budget/m-p/13858958/highlight/true#M3816

 

And finally this blog with all budget options (Custom field with budget % by employee coming from a lookup table is leading practice):

https://community.sap.com/t5/human-capital-management-blogs-by-members/choosing-the-right-budget-configuration-for-your-organization/ba-p/13536803

 

All the best

Xavier

entry.title: Can DM user reschedule orders or set processing time of orders downloaded from ERP?
entry.content:
Dear community members,

 

I hope you are doing well.

I got wondered in Manage Orders app while testing order downloads from HANA Cloud.

 

1. Is it impossible that DM users adjust 'scheduled start's and 'end's point without REO? It seems the boxes are not editable (there are pictures below...). 

 

2. Is it possible to modify Parameters for scheduling such as Setup Time; Processing Time; Teardown Time; etc. of each operation in DM, and how? To be precise, those time parameters unfortunately have been set all zero minute in HANA Cloud, so I will modify those in DM after order downloaded.

For your information, my team is using SAP DM TDD & Hana Cloud Partner Demo Customizing.

I appreciate your comment with opinion in advance.

 

Yours faithfully,

Wonpyo

#digital manufacturing #scheduling modification #processing time

entry.content: Hi Lxiang,

entry.title: Re: Data description import from different file
entry.content:
Hi JoãoPiteira, 

   If economic codes is a dimension, you can make it as a public dimension. With that, you can import the second file with code and its description imported by mapping to the description column of the dimension. 

Best regards, William

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hi @David_Chaviano ,

My name is An. Thank you so much for the amazing opportunity to participate in the digital skills initiative by SAP Learning.

I have been deeply interested in advancing my skills and knowledge in the field of SAP technologies, recognizing the immense value and impact SAP platforms bring to businesses worldwide. I identify myself as a first-generation student and as someone passionate about embracing the latest advancements in this domain, mastering SAP holds personal importance for me as it not only signifies professional growth but also presents an opportunity to promote diversity within industries.

I am eager to write this post to avail myself of the opportunity for a free certification attempt, believing it will enhance my skills and keep me abreast of technological advancements. Additionally, I have completed the course "Creating Queries in SAP BW/4HANA" and am prepared to undertake the corresponding SAP certification.

I am ready to learn more about SAP and to undergo the rigors of studying for SAP. Looking forward to these series of certification attempts!

Thanks & Regards,

An Nguyen.

entry.title: Re: HXEDownloadManager can't run on windows 10

And then, i try to download Server only virtual machine Package but i got Connection timeout when download hxe.ova.007 and hxe.ova.004(i tried download with and without vpn), how can i fix that? Thank u so much, again!

entry.title: Re: Application object BUPA: Table /SCMB/D_BPTMGEN does not exist Message No. R1149
entry.content:
Hello,

Let me understand why you are creating this BP and for what reason?

Generally these are created for SRM or TM purpose. If the answer is yes then you can use the PPOME transaction to create these.

entry.title: Re: Where to fetch the odata Api for successfactors
entry.content:

entry.title: Re: Fetching data from multiple ODATA entities using SuccessFactor Odata V2 adapter in SAP CPI
entry.content: Normally you can exoand the enttities within the queries. I blogged about accessing data in general here (there is a query with expand keyword) https://blog.bajonczak.com/fetching-data-from-sap-successfactors-via-odata-and-oauth/. But keep in mind that SAP does not Support all specific from odata like aggregation or function calls

entry.title: To restrict user to proceed to next steps if the threshold is in "Red" warning symbol
entry.content:
Hi Expert, 

I currently have a requirement from the client, where user want to restrict the business to input to SAC stories if the threshold is in "Red" warning symbol, based on my experience i don't think SAC able to restrict user to restrict them to input any figure if the threshold is in "Red" warning symbol. Diagram below is the example of threshold, if user input 3 or more, it will become warning symbol.

SAP Analytics Cloud for planning SAP Analytics Cloud

entry.title: Re: Problem in POST Exit Implementation of C4C - S4HC Integration package - Create Service Order in
entry.content:
Hello @sivaramaraju_danthuluri 

thank you for your response. The package is

SAP Cloud for Customer Integration with SAP S/4HANA Cloud

This is the message (shortened) that is generated in the EXIT (but of course I could change namespace etc):

<p1:Messages xmlns:p1="http://sap.com/xi/S4CRM/" xmlns:ns0="http://sap.com/xi/XI/SplitAndMerge">
  <p1:Message1>
    <p1:ServiceOrderRequest>
       <MessageHeader>
          <CreationDateTime>2024-09-06T12:29:58.001214Z</CreationDateTime>
       </MessageHeader>
       <ServiceOrder actionCode="01">
           <ServiceOrderType>SVO1</ServiceOrderType>
           <ServiceOrderDescription>TEST Integration V3</ServiceOrderDescription>
                ...
         </ServiceOrder>
     </p1:ServiceOrderRequest>
    </p1:Message1>
</p1:Messages>

2234180 - SAP S/4HANA Application “Manage Your Solution“ - SAP for Me

I'm currently looking into SPRO but haven't found related config so far.

 

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Expansion of Hierarchy Dimension - Drill Level Set to 5
entry.content: Is the limitation increased now ? We are on QRC1 2024 and are still seeing only Level 5. Any config to be changed ?

entry.title: Re: Version management in SAP ERP
entry.content:
This is why, it is normally not recommended to overwite DEV systems. I know lot of projects theese days (because of S/4 upgrades) has this in their scope. But you will loose info!

I know some Companies have build some tools and also there have been a lot of "tricks" during the years. 

And note 130906 is a good starting point - but you need to export before overwriting. So if copy already is done, you are basically "to late".

entry.title: Re: Expansion of Hierarchy Dimension - Drill Level Set to 5
entry.content: Is the limitation increased now ? We are on QRC1 2024 and are still seeing only Level 5. Any config to be changed ? BW shows till Level 10.

entry.title: Find IMG linked to Table TCK35
entry.content: Hi! I am looking for the IMG path or table where table TCK35 is pulling data from. I first sought out transaction code OKK4 but it is only linked to Table TCK05 & TCK06, what I need is to find the IMG path of the plant specific valuation variant i.e. table TCK35.

entry.title: Re: Version management in SAP ERP
entry.content: Yeah, which is why we had made a complete copy of the dev environment to a new instance. So, data is not lost, it's just not where it's supposed to be.

entry.title: Re: Version management in SAP ERP
entry.content: Okay good. If you are the basis team, go for instructions in the note. If not ask the team to do 🙂

entry.title: Re: Alternative options of Group reporting-1SG in SAP S/4...
entry.content:
Dear,

for Scope item 1SG, Public cloud customers should be able to use a number of consolidation units free of charge(possibly 20),

there are a number of alternatives,  you could set up an elimination company code if the elimination scenarios are not complicated. and use Upload Journal Entries up to posting adjusting entries, and run financial reports including elimination company code to view consolidated reports.

another option is to extract financial data to a 3rd party system to do consolidations there

example: Consolidations and Group Reporting | SimpleFi Solutions, LLC (sap.com)

entry.title: Re: Version management in SAP ERP
entry.content: Or you could reconsider if you really need a Refresh of DEV?

entry.title: Multiple PM forms are getting launched when rule get triggered.
entry.content:
Dear All,

When rule is getting triggered multiple PM forms are getting launched for user. We have placed restriction also in Form template setting also. If someone know about this please share the information it will be helpful. Thanks in Advance!

entry.title: Re: SAP extends ERP cloud portfolio with "SAP ERP, private edition, option for Microsoft SQL&qu
entry.content:
@Jayshree_Ravi – let me take the questions one by one.

  • SAP ECC version: Yes, we are talking about a lift & shift from an on-premise SAP ECC 6 system into a SAP-managed private cloud environment running on Microsoft Azure. From a software standpoint customers stay on SAP ECC 6, but in a different operational model where SAP runs the customers system in a cloud environment.
  • Microsoft Cloud Services: As the target operational model runs on Microsoft Azure, customers will be able to use the various innovation services that come with Microsoft Azure natively (i.e. with regards to security, compliance, collaboration, scalability, etc.).
  • Tooling for moving to the cloud: Yes, SAP will offer migration services to execute the lift & shift from on-premise into the cloud.
  • SAP will support all MS SQL Server versions through the maximum Extended Support date. Right now this means supported versions are MS SQL Server 2016 and higher. The supported OS versions go in line with these supported MS SQL Server versions.

entry.title: Unable to drill down TO more than Level 5 in SAC Hierarchy - where BW Hierarchy is Level 10.
entry.content:
When I right click on the Hierarchy it shows only till Level 5, whereas same BW Query level hierarchy shows till Level 10. We are on QRC 1 2024. BW Live connection. IS there any way to show the same as in BW Query. I found SAP release saying, its fixed in SAC. This is a Q4 2022. We are way beyond in SAC QRC1 2024> Why is it not working?

 

BW

entry.title: Re: Problem in POST Exit Implementation of C4C - S4HC Integration package - Create Service Order in
entry.content:
Yes this is a bug, please create an incident we will provide a correction. You can create the bug against the component LOD-CRM-INT-S4H. For faster processing you can mention in the incident to directly assign it to me so that I can ask the person responsible to provide the fix.

Regards, Abinash

entry.title: Re: Direct Amount - Total Eligible Base
entry.content: Hi @xavierlegarrec,

entry.title: Re: Direct Amount - Total Eligible Base
entry.content: Hi @xavierlegarrec, Thank you for your feedback and response, I really appreciate it. I had already gone through these posts before reaching out, as I'm currently helping a client define their budget. To understand how it works, I configured the Budget Amount mode in an internal environment to simulate Budget Cascading, but when I start the Cascading activity, the "Total Eligible Base" field is always zero. For this reason, I wanted to understand how it is populated and whether there is a missing configuration on my end. I couldn't find any configuration steps related to "Total Eligible Base" in the implementation guide. So far, I have set the budget calculation mode to DirectAmount, assigned a value of 100, and set it as 'Based On' the planner. Am I missing something? How is the "Total Eligible Base" calculated? From the documentation, I understood that the Direct Amount is a total budget set by the company, which is then split across different levels of the chain via cascading. That's why I was wondering where it could be entered. Thanks!

entry.title: Re: Batch characteristic value disappear after the change - /SCWM/WM_BATCH_MAINT
entry.content: Hello, Did you manage to find the issue and resolve it? Can you please update the post if possible, Thanks.

entry.title: Re: SAP Enterprise Architecture Training
entry.content:
Hi Rene:

I have a few questions about the prerequisites of the course.

According to the SAP Education site, the essential preprerequisite is "TOGAF foundation knowledge, preferably TOGAF certified".

Does it means that the participants of the course need to take the TOGAF® 9 Foundation course from a The Open Group accredited training course provider or read the TOGAF® 9 Foundation Study Guide, or take the TOGAF® Enterprise Architecture Foundation course from a The Open Group accredited training course provider or read the TOGAF® Enterprise Architecture Foundation Study Guide? If so, can you specify the version of the course or study guide?

Which certification is preferably the participants of the course have?, TOGAF® 9 Foundation, TOGAF® 9 Certified, TOGAF® Enterprise Architecture Foundation, TOGAF® Enterprise Architecture Practitioner or TOGAF® Business Architecture Foundation?

With kind regards,

Rodrigo Monsalve

SAP Consultant

entry.title: Flow Function of the Month: Dismiss Keyboard 🏆
entry.content:
You know that feeling when you have a coding problem and then you find that there exists an object or method or property that gives you the exact thing you need?

I was building an ice breaker app for our next CodeJam (by the way, you can request an SAP Build or any CodeJam anywhere in the world on our CodeJam page). I created a form to let people answer some fun questions about themselves, and then display everyone's answer.

Now when I clicked the "Tell Everybody" button, the data was submitted. But if the user did not explicitly dismiss the keyboard, it remained, and this to me was really annoying, especially since there seemed no button in the keyboard to dismiss itself and it scrolled the page so I couldn't see most of it.

To my good fortune, there is a somewhat lesser known "Dismiss Keyboard" flow function, that does exactly what I needed. You'll have to import from the Marketplace.

And I could put it right after I handle the (successful) submit button click.

Now, after the user submits the form, the keyboard goes away and I can see the entire page again. 

😺

entry.title: Settlements in SuccessFactors Learning
entry.content:
Hi,

I have a question regarding LMS and the billing of training.

Can I close a course immediately after I have triggered a survey and attendances have been maintained, or does this affect the feedback form so that employees can no longer retrieve it? We have always waited 2 weeks before closing a course in the past, but I am wondering if this is actually necessary.

Regards, Monica

entry.title: Re: FreeHand SQL query with input parameters
entry.content:
If the object is a VIEW then try with:

SELECT * FROM CV_MOV_CCEE_RD_WP( 
	PLACEHOLDER.P_MANDT=>'100', 
	PLACEHOLDER.P_DATE_FROM=>'1980-01-01', 
	PLACEHOLDER.P_DATE_TO=>'1980-01-01' 
)

Regards,

entry.title: Re: Enabling cross container access for Nodejs function i...
entry.content:
One of the most straightforward way is by exposing them as HTTP-based services (e.g., REST APIs) and consuming these services in the other container.

In the source container where your Node.js function resides, create an HTTP endpoint that exposes the function.

In the target container that wants to call the function from Container 1, use HTTP client libraries like axios or request in your Node.js code to call the exposed REST API.

Note that Cross-container communication often requires proper authentication and authorization.

entry.title: Blanket Agreement approval not showing to Approver in Mobile App/Desktop App in SAP B1 HANA
entry.content:
Dear All, 

One user has drafted Blanket Agreement/Sales Order, it is going for approval as per approval template, but it is not showing to approver in alert in his mobile app and desktop App. 

I am not able to find out why it is happening. Please help me out.

Regards,

Mohit

entry.title: Error while opening a PDF created by a smartform
entry.content:
Hello,

I'm creating a PDF file using a smartform and then sending it by email.

Everything works great in my DEV environment and I can open the PDF in SOST as well as the receiver mailbox.

For some reason, I can't open the PDF in my QA instance. It's empty. While debugging my code I saw that the execution of the smartform returns an error that doesn't tell me anything useful - " "TERMS_OF_PAYMENT_NOT_IN_T052". It isn't related to the payment terms...

Any idea anyone??... There should be no differences between the output determination between DEV & QA.. at least not something which I'm aware of.

entry.title: Re: Find IMG linked to Table TCK35
entry.content:
Try this for plant specific valuation variant 

IMG-> Controlling (CO) -> Product Cost Controlling -> Cost Object Controlling -> Product Cost by Period -> Manufacturing: Period-End Closing -> Valuation Variant for Order/Process/Production Cost Collector ->

Define Valuation Variants for Order/Process/Production Cost Collector

entry.title: Re: Remove validity characteristic from validity table
entry.content: Validity defines the key in ADSO. You will have to delete the content of ADSO

entry.title: Re: Problem in POST Exit Implementation of C4C - S4HC Integration package - Create Service Order in
entry.content: Ticket is create, thank you very much for your quick support!

entry.title: Re: Third-Party Returns
entry.content:
https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-q-a/third-party-sales-returns-process/qaq-p/3756248Please check https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-q-a/third-party-returns/qaq-p/3277815

and https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-q-a/third-party-sales-returns-process/qaq-p/3756248

The third party returns is not supported by SAP as per the note 751609.

entry.title: Re: SAP BPA Process Started but automation not triggered
entry.content: Hi @CPI_Basis, There are 2 things you could check. 1) Make sure if the Process Automation project(from settings icon) has a version equal to or less than the Desktop agent version 2) The Agent Mode is "Unattended".

entry.title: Re: How to find Process Chain Name from SM37 JOB
entry.content:
You can check in table RSPCPROCESSLOG or RSPCLOGCHAIN.
In the selection screen, input the Job Name you found in SM37.

entry.title: Deactivate Credit Limit Management for TRM Financial Transactions in S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Problem Description:

In S/4HANA Public Cloud, Treasury and Risk Management (TRM), when a financial transaction is being created (e.g. a financial transaction of SAP pre-delivered product type 55A is being created), the credit limit management for counterparty risk is automatically active for this financial transaction (the checkbox ‘CP Risk Active’ is automatically selected on tab Default Risk Limit), and sometimes the “annoying” warning message K-788 'Limit management was not updated' shows up.

Requirement:

The requirement is to deactivate the warning message K-788  'Limit management was not updated' when financial transaction is created or checked, and also deactivate the credit limit management for counterparty risk for financial transactions.

Solution:

1. Access app Financial Objects Substitution Rules - Default Risk Data.

2. Choose Create Rule.

3. On the dialog box Create Rule, choose appropriate Business Context and Event, and then choose Create.

In this example, the objective is to deactivate the credit limit management for financial transactions of product category 550, therefore we choose Business Context Money Market: Default Risk Data and Event Money Market: Default Risk Data.

The full list of Business Context and Event is as below. Please choose the appropriate Business Context and Event according to the product category for which you would like to deactivate credit limit management.

Business Context

Event

Example of Related Product Categories

Bank Accounts: Default Risk Data

Bank Accounts: Default Risk Data

200

Derivatives: Default Risk Data

Derivatives: Default Risk Data

620, 760

Foreign Exchange: Default Risk Data

Foreign Exchange: Default Risk Data

600

Money Market: Default Risk Data

Money Market: Default Risk Data

530, 540, 550, 560, 580

Security Positions: Default Risk Data

Security Positions: Default Risk Data

010, 020, 030, 040, 042, 160

Security Transaction: Default Risk Data

Security Transaction: Default Risk Data

010, 020, 030, 040, 042, 160

Trade Finance: Default Risk Data

Trade Finance: Default Risk Data

850, 860

4. On the next screen, enter the Rule Name, Description, and then specify the precondition and substitution.

Example:

  • Precondition: FinancialInstrProductCategory Equal To 550
  • Substitution: TrsyFinObjCtptyRiskActiveCode Substitute with Constant 0, with ‘Overwrite’ checkbox selected.

This rule means that credit limit management for counterparty risk will be deactivated for financial transaction of product category 550.

5. Save and Activate the rule. Make sure it is in status ‘Active’.

Result:

With this rule activated, the credit limit management for counterparty risk will be deactivated for newly created financial transaction of product category 550 (checkbox ‘CP Risk Active’ is not selected), and the warning message K-788 will not show up when saving or checking the financial transaction of product category 550.

Appendix:

To use this app Financial Objects Substitution Rules - Default Risk Data, please assign a business role containing appropriate business catalog to your user. More details (including how to transport rule between systems) could be found from help document https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/e5ec5859d8e54df98492d80564a734c0/08f3f64e67ea4ea1a785a29d94f85f13.html?locale=en-US

Could you please provide me with the further procedure.

Thank you for your Time.

entry.title: Re: Employee Central - Compensation Promotion integration in Successfactors

Hello @xavierlegarrec 

Thank you for the help.

Another question is, even if the synch rule does work, is this a safe solution to suggest or in any case it is prefferable consulting wise to propose either the standard promotion without the position fields or as a second scenario a custom promotion section which will be followed by massive imports in EC?

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: How to fill the search field using semantic navigatio...
entry.content:
Hi Gregor,

To my knowledge there is no provision currently to pass it to the search box in the target - it only maps the incoming values to the filter properties there! This should be treated as a new requirement if this is a use case.

Best Regards,

    Sandeep

entry.title: Re: Product Reservation for Sales Order
entry.content:
Hi,

Based on your business requirements, I recommend exploring the Product Allocation functionalities available in advanced ATP, which requires an additional license (scope item 1JW). Product Allocation is designed to manage the distribution of limited products to customers or customer groups according to predefined allocations. This process ensures that high-priority customers, regions, or channels receive an appropriate share of available inventory.

For a detailed overview of how Product Allocation operates and the benefits it offers, you can refer to the following resources:

  • SAP S/4HANA Product Allocation Process
  • Product Allocation in advanced Available-to-Promise

Additionally, there are several business add-ins (BAdIs) that allow you to customize and enhance Product Allocation for your specific business needs:

  • ATP Product Allocation Time Series Check
  • ATP Product Allocation Sequence Constraint Preparation
  • ATP Product Allocation Characteristic Value Combination Determination

Regarding the integration between customer downpayments and product allocations in sales orders, you may need to implement custom logic using the appropriate BAdIs or APIs.

I hope this guidance helps you in considering possible directions. It might also be beneficial to seek consulting services from SAP professional services or a project implementation partner for more tailored advice.

Best Regards,
Fancy

entry.title: How to add External Libraries to a SAP Fiori Elements App .
entry.content:
Hi,

I am trying to integrated third party libraries to SAP Fiori Element App.

App is launched via FLP. Its not loading the libraries properly when it is loaded via extended controller file or from Component.JS .

As of now I am trying to load it via onInit or onBeforeRendering to load lets say a google map via CDN.

UI Version : 1.108.23

jQuery.sap.includeScript("https://maps.googleapis.com/maps/api/js?key=""&loading=async&libraries=geometry", 'googleMaps', this.initializeMap.bind(this));

initializeMap: function () {
			var map = new google.maps.Map(this.getView().byId("idmap").getDomRef(), {
				center: { lat: -34.397, lng: 150.644 },
				zoom: 2
			});
		}

Also in a different scenario when app to app navigation is enabled ...unless there is hard refresh on the second app's object page where a third party library is loaded...it is not loading the library.

Wondering if its a Bug or a known issue which is resolved. 

Any recommendations. 

Thanks,

Abhi

entry.title: Re: Credit Line Analysis App is showing busy indicator.
entry.content: Hi, if you do not attach a screenshot with the error log from the F12 console. I can't help further. I propose to open a ticket.

entry.title: Re: Missing BEx Analyzer Icons on Excel´s Add-Ins Menu
entry.content:
This could be happening because the Color Transparency of the EPM Menu items are not aligning with the Windows/Microsoft 365 Theme settings.

 Try this option. Open the Excel and try changing the different Office Theme. You will find the option here File -> Options.  

Restart excel and check if it helps

entry.title: How to Specify Number Assignment Across Company Codes for Fixed Asset in Public Cloud?
entry.content: Is it necessary to specify number assignment across company codes in public cloud? If yes, how to do so? Thank you.

entry.title: Strategic Decision Paths for Managing Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) and Plastic Taxes
entry.content:
Summary

This article provides businesses with a comprehensive guide to making architectural decisions regarding SAP solutions for managing Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) and plastic taxes. It specifically explores into two SAP solutions: SAP Recycling Administration (REA) and SAP Responsible Design and Production (RDP), offering insights into the optimal solution paths based on different customer landscapes and business needs within the context of sustainability and the circular economy.

The guidance includes:

  • For Existing SAP REA Users: Steps and considerations for transitioning to SAP RDP.
  • For New Customers: Guidance on directly implementing SAP RDP, leveraging its latest features and capabilities.

By exploring these aspects, the article aims to equip businesses and IT with the knowledge needed to make informed decisions about managing EPR and plastic taxes during the transition to SAP RDP.

Business Requirements

The management of EPR and plastic taxes as part of a comprehensive sustainability strategy requires meticulous attention to:

  • Regulatory Compliance: Ensure compliance with EPR and plastic tax regulations, addressing both global and regional requirements.
  • Reporting and Documentation: Generating accurate, detailed reports for regulatory bodies, producer responsibility organizations (PROs), and tax authorities to demonstrate compliance.
  • Integration with Existing Systems: Seamlessly integrate with current ERP and business systems to streamline processes.
  • Functionality and Automation: Prioritizing advanced automation features that reduce manual workload, improve data accuracy, and enable intelligent data processing to manage complex regulatory scenarios.
  • Sustainability and Future-readiness: Selecting solutions that not only meet current needs but are scalable to adapt to future sustainability initiatives, including reducing environmental impact and supporting circular economy practices.

Solution Options

SAP provides two main solution options for managing EPR and plastic taxes within the scope of Sustainability initiatives:

  • SAP Recycling Administration (REA): The REA component is integrated within the standard SAP ECC and S/4HANA systems but requires activation of the business function REA – Recycling Administration (J_7L_REA_CORE). To fully utilize REA, the components Materials Management (MM) and Sales and Distribution (SD) must be implemented. REA is a specific solution designed for handling EPR-related obligations, particularly in the European market. It offers a stable solution with specific country-based configurations but lacks global applicability and no future roadmap.
  • SAP Responsible Design and Production (RDP): A modern cloud-based solution designed to support global EPR, plastic packaging taxes, and circular economy initiatives. It offers a comprehensive approach with advanced features like real-time analytics, integrated reporting for relevant producer responsibility organizations (PROs), tax authorities, and continuous updates to support evolving regulations. Additionally, RDP provides standard APIs to connect seamlessly with SAP S/4HANA, enabling smooth integration and data exchange. For a detailed list of available APIs, you can visit  Overview | SAP Responsible Design and Production | SAP Business Accelerator Hub

 

Characterization of the Solution Options

To provide a clear understanding of the differences between SAP Responsible Design and Production (RDP) and SAP Recycling Administration (REA), we will characterize each solution based on its capabilities and alignment with regulatory requirements and sustainability goals. This comparison will help determine which solution best fits your organization’s needs.

SAP Responsible Design and Production (RDP)

  • Regulatory Calculations: Supports global packaging EPR and plastic packaging taxes, providing detailed analytics and forecasting capabilities.
  • Compliance: Aligns with global regulations and voluntary commitments, ensuring businesses meet or exceed regulatory expectations.
  • Real-time Updates: Offers continuous development and updates, keeping the system aligned with changing regulatory landscapes.
  • Integration: Seamless integration with SAP PLM, ERP, and other systems through APIs, supporting a unified approach to data management.
  • Data Quality: Ensures data quality and consistency with automated validations and corrections, reducing the risk of errors.
  • Technology Platform: Hosted on SAP BTP, enabling scalable, secure, and innovative features that integrate seamlessly with other SAP and non-SAP systems.

SAP Recycling Administration (REA)

  • EPR Obligations: Supports calculation of EPR obligations for specific materials, primarily focused on the European market.
  • Maintenance Mode: Currently in maintenance mode with no new feature development, limited to bug fixes and support until 2030 for ECC and 2040 for S/4HANA.
  • Integration: Available on S/4HANA and integrates with standard SAP functions, with a strong reliance on traditional ERP modules like SD and MM.
  • Automation: Provides automatic determination of quantity flow from Sales and Distribution (SD) and Materials Management (MM), but lacks the advanced automation features of RDP.
  • Cost Control: Utilizes Financial Accounting (FI), Controlling (CO), and Sales and Distribution (SD) for budgeting and controlling recycling costs, allowing for comprehensive cost management.
  • Certified Verification: Fulfills country-specific reporting requirements with recycling partners, providing certified verification at the level of individual documents and items.

Criteria to determine the most suitable target solution

When selecting the most suitable SAP solution for managing Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) schemes and plastic taxes, it is essential to consider several key criteria. These factors will help guide your decision between SAP Responsible Design and Production (RDP) and SAP Recycling Administration (REA).

  • Regulatory Compliance Needs: Assess whether the business needs comprehensive regulatory compliance for global EPR and plastic taxes, which RDP is better suited for, or if a focus on European schemes through REA suffices.
  • Functionality and Automation: Determine the necessity for advanced features such as automated data acquisition, intelligent data processing, and advanced reporting capabilities. RDP provides a more intelligent and automated approach to managing EPR and plastic taxes, while REA is limited to basic reporting and manual data processes.
  • Data Accessibility and Integration: Evaluate the required level of data accessibility and integration with existing systems. RDP offers modern API-based integrations, whereas REA relies on more traditional approaches. 
  • Operational Requirements: Identify specific operational needs such as reporting, documentation, and real-time data management. RDP provides more advanced and automated solutions compared to REA
  • Maintenance and Updates: Consider the need for ongoing updates and maintenance support. RDP, as a SaaS solution, offers continuous updates, new features, and enhanced analytics, ensuring alignment with evolving regulatory requirements. In contrast, REA is in maintenance mode, with no new feature development—only bug fixes and compliance updates—until its end of maintenance in 2030 for ECC and 2040 for S/4HANA. Organizations should weigh these timelines and technological differences when deciding between the two solutions. 
  • Technology Platform Compatibility: Evaluate if the solution needs to be cloud-based (RDP on SAP BTP) or if an on-premise solution (REA) meets current IT and sustainability goals.

 

Evaluation Summary

Refer to the evaluation details below.

Criteria/Options

SAP RDP

SAP REA

Regulatory Calculations

Global EPR and plastic packaging taxes

EPR obligations for specific materials (Europe-focused)

Compliance

Global regulations and voluntary commitments

Country-specific compliance

Real-time Updates

Continuous updates and new features

No new updates, only bug fixes

Integration with SAP Systems

Seamless integration via APIs

Traditional integration with standard SAP functions

Maintenance Mode

Actively maintained and updated

In maintenance mode, no new features

Comprehensive Data Management

Automated validations, ensures data quality

Manual checks required, limited automation

Intelligent Data Processing

Advanced analytics and real-time monitoring

Basic reporting, limited to manual processes

EPR and Plastic Tax Analytics

Advanced analytics, detailed reporting

Basic fixed reports, limited analytics

System Connectivity

API-based integration with various systems via SAP BTP

Requires development for third-party connections

 

Decision Tree

A simple decision tree to select the appropriate solution for managing EPR schemes and plastic tax management is illustrated below.

 

 

Conclusion

In conclusion, selecting the appropriate SAP solution for managing EPR and plastic taxes depends on your organization's specific requirements and existing SAP environment.

Implementation Recommendations Based on Existing Customer Landscapes:

  1. New Customers: Implement SAP RDP directly to take advantage of its robust regulatory compliance features and seamless integration with existing SAP systems.
  2. Existing SAP REA Users: Consider two paths:
  1. Transition to SAP RDP to leverage comprehensive EPR and plastic tax management capabilities and benefit from continuous updates and integration with SAP’s latest offerings.
  2. SAP S4HANA Logistic General -Returnable Packaging and Empties Management - This is an adaptation of the existing ECC-REA application on S4HANA. It provides similar functionalities and can be implemented as a brownfield conversion approach, where existing REA customers can transition while retaining their current investment. For organizations considering this path, it's important to note that REA will reach its end of maintenance in 2030 for ECC and 2040 for S/4HANA.

By carefully evaluating the solutions and considering the specific needs and requirements, businesses and IT can make informed decisions and ensure effective management of EPR and plastic taxes in alignment with sustainability goals.

www.sap.com.

entry.title: Re: Find IMG linked to Table TCK35
entry.content: Good day. After Product Cost by Period, no Manufacturing: Period End Closing is found. expanded further, Define Valuation Variants for Order/Process/Production Cost Collector is also not found.

entry.title: Hello,how to create a field to disability and degree of disability in Global Info
entry.content:
Hello , for my client i have to create a field  to disability and degree of disability in Global Info,create this field in Core and ONB? what is the action i shoud take can anyone help me

Thank you

entry.title: Re: Unable to drill down TO more than Level 5 in SAC Hier...
entry.content:
Hello Rohan,

unfortunately SAC Updates often don't work indenpendently, but it might be that you need another note in your SAP BW backend system.

Check this note: https://me.sap.com/notes/2541557   (Program: Z_SAP_BW_NOTE_ANALYZER).

And try to find out, if there is one or more which can be implemented in your SAP BW release.

BR, Martin

entry.title: Re: Download Signavio insights output into PDF/Word format
entry.content:
Dear Subramanya,

It has been brought to our attention that there are still open questions to be answered in this channel. We apologise for our late response to your message. Please find the following answer:

You can only download data from SAP Signavio Process Insights as a CSV file. The download option allows you to share the data with colleagues and perform additional analyses offline, e.g. in a spreadsheet application such as Excel. To view the CSV file in a readable format, you need to import the file into your spreadsheet and check the settings of the spreadsheet application to ensure that the semicolon used as a separator in the CSV file is also used in the spreadsheet application.

Hope this helps. If you have further questions, please do not hesitate to contact us via signavio.success@sap.com

Kind regards

Ascha (Virtual Customer Success Team - SAP Signavio)

entry.title: Re: HXEDownloadManager can't run on windows 10
entry.content: here is log detail: D:\Server_SAP_HANA\hxe.ova.007 - Connection timeout. Retry... D:\Server_SAP_HANA\hxe.ova.004 - Connection timeout. Retry...

entry.title: Loop/Iterate in the SAP Build Process Autoamtion Process flow
entry.content:
I am looking for a way to use loops or iterate calling an action in the process.

I am aware of the looping fucntionality and other conditions available in the automation, but currently I am looking for loops in the Process alone, that way not having to use automation/desktop agent.


Please let me know if the functionality is already present or if there can be any workarounds that I can use.
Thanks in advance

entry.title: Re: How to convert a Sales Quote to Sales Order on Sales...
entry.content:
Hi Eduardo,

An integrated Quote to Order process as we know or expect it from V1 does not yet seem to exist in V2. The transactions look to exist quite independent from one another. I do see changes in the different versions of the documentation so SAP is working on it.

Please check chapter 22 Sales Orders for example of the latest version of the solution guide : https://help.sap.com/doc/300d71dc3d2141fb8941453f95b85541/CSS_SHIP/en-US/CX_NG_CSS_SalesSolGuide.pdf

  • SAP Sales Cloud Version 2 helps you record and view sales interactions related to sales orders, and it offers
    three types of maintenance modes for sales orders:
    • Internal: You can create, search, view, modify, and complete sales orders in SAP Sales Cloud Version 2.
    • Internal & External: You can create, search, view, modify sales orders in SAP Sales Cloud Version 2,
    simulate them in SAP S/4HANA, transfer them to SAP S/4HANA, and fulfill them in SAP S/4HANA. On
    simulation, you receive results of penny perfect pricing, credit check, and so on. Sales orders with this
    maintenance mode are also called bidirectional sales orders.
    • External: You can replicate sales orders from SAP S/4HANA or SAP ERP, and you can search and view the
    sales orders in SAP Sales Cloud Version 2.

Nowhere it is mentioned in the documentation that after submit or after (external) pricing is exectued succesfully or at a certain phase the sales quote can be converted into a sales order. Nor is there a 'related items' area where a sales order can be linked to an existing sales quote.

You can though create and view an external follow-up SAP S/4HANA sales order from sales quotes. When you have set up integration you should be able to visualize a Sales Orders Section in which you can select + to create an external sales order.

As changes come along fast i would consider the Whats new link on a regular basis as you can easily filter on Sales Quotes and Sales Orders to see what is happening:

What's New in SAP Sales Cloud Version 2 | SAP Help Portal

I would also advise you to create an incident, mention your aimed quote to order process and ask SAP what is possible now and what is upcoming in this process.

Kr.

MJ

entry.title: Re: Inconsistent Replication Flow Behavior in SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
Hello @wsamuel 

unfortunately you don't write what system is your source? Is it S/4HANA, which release?

And most important if so ... have you checked and implemented all the notes in SE38 program: CNV_NOTE_ANALYZER_DI  (see: https://me.sap.com/notes/3016862)

This is really vital... since there have been so many notes for Replication Flows during the 2023, until Jun 2024.

I try to keep my affected S4 system always up-to-date and luckily found no issues during the last months.

BR, Martin

entry.title: Re: HXEDownloadManager can't run on windows 10
entry.content: Hi Lxiang, i just try close HXEDownloadMgr and open again, and Its work. Thank u so much!

entry.title: Re: Onboarding (2.0) with an External HRIS
entry.content:
Hi Smita, this is a very interesting topic.

I myself have implemented this in the past successfully and I have a new scenario now where Employee ID is generated in SF and the same flows to ECC. Then we have to update the Employee data from ECC to SF which we are doing currently but there is an issue, since the employee ID generated already used for Onboardee (External User) cannot be the same for the Employee file synced from ECC (Internal User). What do you suggest?

Thanks in advance.

Surya Alluri

entry.title: Re: Reorganizing of demand with T-code MD74 is not working
entry.content: For example, you have a demand of 100. This you will check in md62 or md63 and for that you have planned orders also. You want to delete the demand for a particular material and set the planned order to 0. When you run this transaction you set the demand to 0 （The PIR requirement date before the key date）, and when you refresh md04 the planned orders will have exception message "26" Excess in individual segment. When you run the MRP these planned orders will be deleted. It is completely deleted form the data base.

entry.title: Re: Data Migration object in S/4 HANA Cloud-Supplier extended
entry.content: Hi Kane, hope this link helps: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/f86dc2eb1f8b48c880a7607213104b27/a67653361d4549698d31490585b38423.html

entry.title: Re: Can not use replication flows to replicate CDS Views...
entry.content:
Hello @sophie2024 

unfortunately you don't write if you have checked and implemented all the notes in SE38 program: CNV_NOTE_ANALYZER_DI  (see: https://me.sap.com/notes/3016862)

This is really vital... since there have been so many notes for Replication Flows during the year 2023, until Jun. 2024. The bad thing is, SAP does not really communicate this pro-actively 😪 There should be regular messages in Datasphere, just like when there are new functions...

I try to keep my affected S4 system always up-to-date and luckily found no issues during the last months.

BR, Martin

entry.title: Hello experts! is there limitations in public cloud to get automatic usage decision working in QM.
entry.content:
Working in public loud project, there are master data set for GR for PO, sampling schemes and MIC's created. Also Dynamic modification is created for automatic usage decision after successfully recorded results. Still no automatic after recorded results. When adding modification rule to material master same issue. When adding modification rule to task list same result. Please advise how to proceed with this. 

BR. JS

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hello @David_Chaviano ,

I hope you are well,

I'm an SAP MM Functional consultant from Egypt. I've been working in SAP for 3 Years Now. I have Knowledge on SAP S4 HANA Sourcing and Procurement but not certified.

I identified as a person in the below categories:
-People in remote or underserved locations that may be not have access to the same economic opportunities as their urban or more privileged counterparts.
-first-generation college graduate in my family.
-People looking to switch their careers mid-life.

I love working with SAP. I want to keep learning and growing in this field for a lifetime to have a great career opportunity.

I will be very grateful if I take a free certification attempt because I believe it would help me to learn more knowledge in SAP and get more opportunities in my career.

Thanks a lot for this grateful Opportunity!

best Regards,

entry.title: Re: Is is possible to enlarge the image by clicking in a published hub journey?
entry.content:
Dear Shreenith,

It has been brought to our attention that there are still open questions to be answered in this channel. We apologise for our late response to your message. Please find the following answer:
To view the complete diagram on your screen or enlarge specific elements, use can use the zoom icons. Please find a further description in our SAP Signavio Process Manager User Guide here: Move and change elements | SAP Help Portal

Hope this helps. For further questions, feel free to comment here or to directly reach out to signavio.success@sap.com

Kind regards

Ascha (Virtual Customer Success Team - SAP Signavio)

entry.title: SAP RAP Custom entity shows correct value in excel download but on screen value is incorrect
entry.content:
SAP RAP Custom entity shows correct value in excel download but on screen value is incorrect. In debug mode, the io_response->set_data( lt_final_response ). is having correct value but once the value shows on fiori screen it is duplicating the second row value in 1st row but when I download to excel, the values are coming correctly.Attaching the screenshot of debug model, fiori screen and excel value for reference.

  1.  Output in debug mode: io_response->set_data( lt_final_response ).

     

  2.  Output of Fiori app: 2nd row value is duplicated in row one

 

  1. But when I download in excel , it shows the output as Output of debug mode which I am also expecting on the Fiori app display but it is not showing correctly.

 

 

 

ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model

entry.title: Re: Schedule background job in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud S...
entry.content:
Also official documentation at help.sap.com:

Creating a Job Catalog Entry and a Job Template in ADT | SAP Help Portal

(Home > SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) > SAP Business Technology Platform > Development > Development in the ABAP Environment > Released Components and Objects > Application Jobs)

(this documentation talks about: IF_APJ_DT_EXEC_OBJECT, IF_APJ_RT_EXEC_OBJECT, CL_APJ_DT_CREATE_CONTENT)

NB: if needed, use CL_APJ_RT_API for Maintaining Application Jobs Using an API (SAP Help Portal).

entry.title: SAP HANA Certification
entry.content:
Hello, 

I am writing to express my keen interest in obtaining an SAP certification to enhance my professional skills and career prospects within the mining industry. I am currently employed as a Data Capture Clerk at Northam Platinum, where I have been responsible for creating work orders and placing orders through SAP systems, specifically IW21, ME51N, and MB21.

While I have gained valuable experience and practical knowledge in these areas, I believe that obtaining an SAP certification would significantly elevate my qualifications and open up new opportunities within the mining sector. A certification would validate my expertise and provide me with a recognized credential that would enhance my credibility as a professional.

As a single mother, I am committed to advancing my career and providing a better future for my family. I believe that obtaining an SAP certification would be a crucial step towards achieving my professional goals and securing a more stable and rewarding career path.

I would be grateful for any guidance or assistance you can provide in pursuing an SAP certification. Please let me know what additional information or documentation may be required to initiate the application process.

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: Mass-activating multiple objects (in background)
entry.content:
Thanks Andreas,

Thanks for your sharing.

It helps us a lot.

🙂

Regards,

ZFiori

entry.title: Re: VBOF sap rebates agreement
entry.content: Hi, Refere KONA as a tabel not a field, KONA table is saving data of all agreements and you find the list of all agreements. If required you generate the report based on KONA table. Regards

entry.title: Re: Data migration - Automatic mapping in "Migrate your data" app SAP S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content: You can migrate the PIRs in the same migration project, in which you migrate the Supplier, so you can re-use the mappings.

entry.title: Filtering with or does not work on different expressions on unmanaged cds
entry.content:
Hi all! I have a custom cds and I am using it as a searchelp in fiori based on application. I have two expressions in this cds which are 'Business Partner' and 'Business Partner Full Name'. In searchelp I have one search bar that's why I have to send the request like business_partner_full_name,'EN' or business_partner,'EN'  but it doesn't work. There is a method called combine_with_or but I can't use for different expressions. Can you help me if there is a solution or another way that can be done with this class? I'm adding the screenshot of combine_with_or method. Appreciate help from everyone in the community. Thanks a lot!

SAP BTP, ABAP environment

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hello @David_Chaviano,

My name is Ninh and I am 30 years old.

I identify myself as: People looking to switch their careers mid-life.

I have just finished the learning journey "Describing the Payables Management Process in SAP S/4HANA". In the future, I want to boost my career as an FICO consultant, especially in AP module.

Here the completion picture of the learning journey:

I hope that you will consider my request.

Thank you!

entry.title: Re: Flow Function of the Month: Dismiss Keyboard 🏆
entry.content:
Great idea, thanks for sharing. 

A note that you could actually attach the logic to the 'onBlur' event node of the input field (or even onSumbit) to auto-dismiss when user taps anywhere away in the UI so that they could also even see their own answers easily before sending them over.

entry.title: Re: Mass update a field in Z*table
entry.content: See this possibility:

entry.title: Folder sharing SAP B1 Cloud control center
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

Could you provide any advice or best practices on setting up restrictions or managing folder access for each reseller in the SAP B1 Cloud Control Center? Specifically, I’d appreciate guidance on how to efficiently control permissions and ensure data segregation for different resellers.

 

Thank you.
